children.text: Daytime Tour At The National Leprechaun Museum
Join the museum's storytellers and travel deep into the world of Irish folklore

Enter the world of the Leprechaun, as you discover the fascinating history of an Irish tradition thousands of years old, that continues to influence mythology the world over. The National Leprechaun Museum, the first ever attraction dedicated to Irish mythology, opens up a fun and magical world full of fascinating folklore and enchanting stories.

Based in the heart of Dublin, you will explore the museum on a guided tour with one of their engaging storytellers. You'll explore spaces that reflect these stories, filled with experiences typically associated with leprechauns.

Feel what it’s like to journey deep beneath the rocks of the Giant’s Causeway, open up your minds to the sights and stories of Ireland’s mythical otherworld on a trip to a fairy hill, find yourself in a leprechaun-sized world and take a journey to the end of the rainbow to see if the elusive crock of gold really exists.

Tour length: approximately 45 minutes. Valid ID may be required for senior, student or teenager tickets. Family tickets provide entry for 2 adults and 2 children. Visitors must arrive 10 minutes before start time – no refunds for late arrivals.

Children must be accompanied by an adult at all times. The experience is not suitable for some younger children and all tours are performed in English. Wisitors must arrive 10 minutes before start time – no refunds for late arrivals

*Find out more in our Terms & Conditions.
